chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَلْبَسُ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهُ لِبَاسُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 83 – What the one in Ihraam can wear, from the clothes, and what is disliked for him, his clothing

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/281)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/281)

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ وَ

text: إِنَّمَا كُرِهَ ذَلِكَ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يَزُرَّهُ الْجَاهِلُ فَأَمَّا الْفَقِيهُ فَلَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَلْبَسَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> similar to that, and he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘But rather, that (wearing of a pillion) is disliked out of fear that the ignorant one would button it. But, as for the understanding one, so there is no problem if he were to wear it’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy from abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, a similar Hadith and said that he called it undesirable because of fear that an ignorant person may button it, but in the case of a faqih it is not harmful. H 7178, Ch. 83, h 9
